questions.q: What is the name of the first book of the Bible?

How many times did Jesus pray in the Garden of Gethsemane before his arrest?
Which book of the Bible is primarily composed of poetry and songs?
In which city was Jesus born according to the Gospels?
What did the Israelites eat during their forty years of wandering in the desert?

In which river did John the Baptist baptize Jesus?
Which book of the Bible concludes with the romantic line about a beloved fleeing like a roe upon mountains of spices?
Complete this famous verse: 'For God so loved the world that he gave his only begotten son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish but have...'
What did the Israelites craft and worship while Moses was receiving the Ten Commandments?
Who was the Roman governor who ordered the crucifixion of Jesus?
On which day of the week does the Bible prescribe that people observe the Sabbath?
What is the first book of the New Testament?
How many commandments were given to Moses on Mount Sinai?
What was the occupation of the criminal who asked Jesus to remember him in paradise?
Who led the Israelites into the Promised Land after Moses died?
What was the name of Noah's wife mentioned in the Bible?
How many days and nights did Jesus spend in the tomb before his resurrection?
What did Jesus transform water into at the wedding feast in Cana?
Which woman anointed Jesus's feet with perfume before his crucifixion?
choices: Both Mary Magdalene and Mary of Bethany

questions.q: How many days did it take God to create the world according to Genesis?
What was the name of the giant defeated by David with a stone?
What did Noah use to build his ark?
Who was Moses' brother that helped him lead the Israelites?
What fruit did Adam and Eve eat in the Garden of Eden?
choices: Fruit from the tree of knowledge

questions.q: How many plagues did God send upon Egypt?
What was the name of Job's wife?

For how many pieces of silver did Judas betray Jesus?

Which book of the Bible contains the story of Job and his trials?
Which book of the Bible is the longest?
Which biblical figure was known for his great strength and was betrayed by Delilah?
In the New Testament, what was the name of the tax collector from Jericho who Jesus visited?

What was the occupation of Lydia, the first European convert to Christianity mentioned in Acts?
In the Book of Daniel, what were the names of the three men cast into the fiery furnace?
choices: Both sets of names refer to the same three men

questions.q: Which apostle was specifically called 'the disciple whom Jesus loved' in the Gospel of John?
What miraculous sign did God provide to King Hezekiah to confirm that he would recover from his illness?
choices: The sun moved backward ten degrees
Fire came down from the sky

questions.q: Which Old Testament figure built an altar and offered a sacrifice after seeing an angel of the Lord appear to him near the threshing floor of Araunah?

In the genealogy of Jesus presented in Matthew's Gospel, how many generations are listed from Abraham to Jesus?
In the book of Job, what does Job's wife urge him to do in response to his suffering?
choices: Build an altar to the Lord

questions.q: Which Old Testament book contains the phrase 'vanity of vanities; all is vanity'?

What was the profession of Amos before he became a prophet?

Which book of the New Testament contains Paul's discussion of the 'thorn in the flesh'?
According to 1 Kings, how many wives did Solomon have?
What was the relationship between Mary Magdalene and the other Mary at Jesus's tomb in Matthew's account?
choices: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joseph

questions.q: In the book of Leviticus, what is the Hebrew term for the scapegoat sent into the wilderness?

choices: The text does not specify the location

questions.q: In Proverbs, what is the first attribute listed in the famous acrostic poem describing a woman of noble character?

Which apostle was specifically chosen to take the Gospel to the Gentiles, as affirmed by the Jerusalem Council in Acts 15?
In the book of Ecclesiastes, what repeated phrase serves as the author's refrain about the futility of earthly pursuits?
choices: What does it profit a man
Vanity of vanities, all is vanity
To everything there is a season
There is nothing new under the sun

questions.q: In the Book of Ruth, what was the name of the Moabite woman who married Boaz and became the great-grandmother of King David?

What was the name of the centurion whose servant Jesus healed without going to his house?

Which king of Israel had a vineyard that Ahab coveted and orchestrated the owner's death to obtain it?
choices: King Ahab fought for Jezebel's vineyard

questions.q: In the Book of Genesis, what was the name of the mountain where Abraham was commanded to sacrifice Isaac?
According to the Book of Hebrews, Jesus is described as a high priest according to the order of which biblical figure?

choices: Peter; the dispersed believers throughout Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia
James; communities in Judea and Samaria
John; the seven churches of Asia Minor
Paul; the churches in Macedonia and Achaia

questions.q: In what year was the King James Version of the Bible first published?

choices: To provide food for the priests
To test the high priest's faith
To symbolically carry away the people's sins

questions.q: What did Samson lose when Delilah betrayed him to the Philistines?

What was the name of the garden where Jesus was arrested the night before his crucifixion?

choices: Not applicable; he was always called Abraham

questions.q: In the Book of Ruth, who was Ruth's first husband before he died?

choices: A fleece that was wet with dew while the ground was dry

questions.q: Which letter in the New Testament was written by Paul to Philemon regarding a runaway slave?

choices: A royal banquet displaying the riches of his kingdom

choices: Path, rocks, thorns, and good soil

questions.q: What was the original name of Saul before God changed it to reflect his new calling as an apostle?
